content_of_post_to_answer: Do you believe people often make their own setbacks harder by overthinking the situation?
answer: Overthinking often becomes a second problem layered on top of the first. The situation may be difficult, but repeatedly replaying it can turn a temporary setback into a permanent identity: “this always happens to me.” The useful distinction is between reflection that creates a next step and rumination that only creates more pain. I've explored this pattern in more detail on my profile, if it helps. The mind can be a tool for solving problems, but it can also become the place where problems keep growing.
